articles.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
articles.description: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

articles.title: Edtech platform raises $4.5M to help teach students how to vibe code
articles.description: Imagi announced a $4.5 million seed round, with investors including Brighteye Ventures, Day One Capital, and artist Will.i.am.

articles.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
articles.description: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

articles.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
articles.description: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

articles.title: Amazon is bringing games to Prime Video
articles.description: Amazon is hoping the addition of games can turn Prime Video into a one-stop entertainment destination, borrowing a strategy from Netflix, which has increasingly embraced party games over the past several years.

articles.title: MeBeMe’s new ‘interrupter’ app helps you become your best self instead of doomscrolling
articles.description: MeBeMe delivers small tasks to help users become their best selves instead of endlessly scrolling.

articles.title: Your backstage pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 is waiting
articles.description: Volunteer at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get a behind-the-scenes look at how one of the world's biggest startup conferences gets built.

articles.title: Ford bets on Apple for its next-generation of EVs
articles.description: Ford is the first automaker to use a new set of developer tools that will let it embed Apple Maps navigation and mapping directly into its vehicles' infotainment systems.

articles.title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
articles.description: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.

articles.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
articles.description: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

articles.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
articles.description: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

articles.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
articles.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

articles.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
articles.description: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

articles.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
articles.description: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.

articles.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
articles.description: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.

articles.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown
articles.description: SoundCloud has acquired decentralized music platform Nina Protocol, months after the startup announced it would shut down. The deal brings Nina’s artists, editorial archive, and music discovery tools to SoundCloud as the company continues expanding its platform for independent musicians.

articles.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
articles.description: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

articles.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
articles.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

articles.title: Science Corporation’s vision-restoring chip wins EU approval
articles.description: "The thing that the space needs is a company making $100 million a year of revenue," Science Corp. CEO Max Hodak said.

articles.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
articles.description: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo sharing, and AI features designed to strengthen real-world relationships.

articles.title: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
articles.description: Aggressive training techniques sharpens threat of bad behavior by leading models.

articles.title: White House report says Trump can usher in a "new golden age" of science
articles.description: The resulting report is a near-random mixture of grievances and real problems.

articles.title: I wanted a clock that never needed setting. Things escalated.
articles.description: Sure, maybe your clock has a backup battery, but mine has a deployment pipeline!

articles.title: Watch orcas ram a sunfish so hard it explodes
articles.description: “We think this may help younger orcas feed more easily, or it could also just be for fun.”

articles.title: Sales were up at Tesla but so were costs and spending
articles.description: Q2 2026 was profitable, but barely.

articles.title: Microsoft brings original Xbox backward compatibility to Windows PCs
articles.description: First four compatible titles can be run with an 11-year-old graphics card.

articles.title: ISPs' long nightmare of having to list all the fees they charge is finally over
articles.description: FCC lets ISPs stop listing all fees after companies complained it was too hard.

articles.title: Clayface trailer leans into the body horror
articles.description: "The people I trusted betrayed me. The justice system failed me."

articles.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
articles.description: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.

articles.title: Next Space Force chief throws cold water on the idea of space privateers
articles.description: It turns out some of Benjamin Franklin's opinions from 240 years ago are still relevant today.

articles.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
articles.description: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

articles.title: What happens when you try to chop a photon in half?
articles.description: A mirror that moves mid-photon could release a shower of new photons.

articles.title: Samsung unveils new Galaxy Z8 foldables and Galaxy Watches, available August 7
articles.description: Samsung's new foldables are slimmer, faster, and more expensive.

articles.title: Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
articles.description: Troops received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens.

articles.title: Ukrainian drones deliver robots directly into battle by sea and air
articles.description: Ukraine's battlefield surge of robots now features airdrops and beach assaults.

articles.title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
articles.description: The Range Rover GT will debut as an EV with a hybrid version to follow.

articles.title: On the run for 20 years, most-wanted fugitive caught hiding as a biotech exec
articles.description: Ronald Fischer, aka Richard Graydon, was arrested in New York last week.

articles.title: Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
articles.description: "Maybe that's my responsibility, to live alone with the truth."

articles.title: Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
articles.description: Taylor Farms stirred confusion on FDA test and provided a vague recall list.

articles.title: Nintendo says users voluntarily paid higher prices, have no right to tariff refunds
articles.description: Nintendo says Switch buyers got what they paid for, urges court to dismiss lawsuit.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI芯片初创公司Etched估值破百亿美元**：尽管面临质疑，Etched仍获知名投资者支持，估值达103亿美元，显示AI芯片领域持续火热。
2. **教育科技平台获450万美元融资**：该平台旨在教授学生“氛围编程”（vibe code），探索编程教育的新模式。
3. **英伟达将GPU送往月球**：英伟达宣布向月球发送GPU，可能用于太空计算或月球任务中的AI处理，拓展芯片应用边界。
4. **Google Gemini接近10亿用户里程碑**：谷歌AI助手Gemini用户数快速增长，即将突破10亿大关，反映AI产品普及加速。
5. **亚马逊将游戏引入Prime Video**：亚马逊计划在Prime Video平台加入游戏功能，进一步整合娱乐生态与订阅服务。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI芯片初创公司Etched逆势获得高估值，凸显市场对专用AI算力的强烈需求，而英伟达进军月球则标志着AI硬件正从地球向太空延伸。

articles.description: OpenAI and Hugging Face share early findings from a security incident during AI model evaluation, highlighting advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

articles.title: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC
articles.description: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC, bringing global leadership in finance, technology, and governance.

articles.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
articles.description: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

articles.title: A scorecard for the AI age
articles.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

articles.title: Why teens deserve access to safe AI
articles.description: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.

articles.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortunate, because most enterprises cannot yet see their unit economics clearly: GPUs sit at half utilizati

articles.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whethe

articles.title: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the fix, but most are still building it; the field is converging on hybrid retrieval; and even as provider

articles.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
articles.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to production on automated evaluation alone — with no human in the loop. The result is an evaluation gap —

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **模型与工具更新**：Hugging Face 推出 Nunchaku 4-bit 扩散模型推理集成，支持更高效的低精度图像生成；Google 发布多项更新，包括 Galaxy Unpacked 2026 的 AI 功能、搜索应用连接扩展、以及 Google Vids 视频编辑与创作新功能。
2. **物理AI与机器人数据**：Hugging Face 发布物理AI仿真现状综述，并推出 Grabette 开源系统，用于记录机器人操作数据，推动机器人学习与仿真研究。
3. **企业与社会应用**：OpenAI 报道 NTT DATA 集团利用 Codex 将事件分析时间缩短至30分钟；推出 ChatGPT 小企业计划；与 Hugging Face 合作应对模型评估中的安全事件；新闻机构通过AI提升使命；OpenAI 还宣布在埃芬汉县建设AI基础设施，并推进国家科学新阶段。
4. **安全与治理**：OpenAI 发布关于长周期模型的安全与对齐研究，并宣布 David Vélez 和 Robin Vince 加入 OpenAI 基金会及集团董事会，同时推出 OpenAI Presence 新功能。
5. **行业合作与扩展**：OpenAI 与 Hugging Face 联合处理安全事件，多个组织通过AI提升效率与创新能力，显示AI在商业、科研和公共领域的加速渗透。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：Hugging Face 推出 Nunchaku 4-bit 扩散模型推理，标志着低精度AI模型在图像生成领域的实用化突破，同时 OpenAI 与 Hugging Face 在安全事件上的合作凸显行业对AI治理的重视。
